channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fans Misinterpret BTS Jungkook’s Instagram Poem Post as Breakup Joke
item.description: BTS member Jungkook drew widespread attention online after sharing a recent Instagram post that unexpectedly sparked humorous speculation among fans. The post featured an excerpt from the poem “Fallin...

item.title: Hosts of Charlie Kirk Show express concerns over Pride Month and societal changes
item.description: On the first day of Pride Month, hosts of The Charlie Kirk Show, Andrew Kolvet and Blake Neff, expressed strong objections to Pride celebrations, linking LGBTQ visibility to concerns about fertility r...

item.title: Bafana Bafana depart for Mexico after visa delays postpone World Cup trip by one day
item.description: Bafana Bafana finally departed from OR Tambo International Airport on Monday afternoon for Mexico ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup after administrative problems delayed the team's planned departure by...

item.title: Sara Duterte asks Senate impeachment court to dismiss case citing constitutional and procedural defects
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte has formally asked the Philippine Senate, sitting as an impeachment court, to dismiss the articles of impeachment filed against her, arguing that they are fundamentally fla...

item.title: Senate Republicans face legislative deadlock over contested Trump-backed funding provisions
item.description: Senate Republicans returned to Capitol Hill on Monday facing mounting political and legislative challenges tied to President Donald Trump’s agenda, as internal divisions and procedural obstacles compl...

item.title: Trump administration ends Anti-Weaponization Fund amid court order and legal scrutiny
item.description: According to reporting cited by Axios and summarized in the article, the Trump administration is moving to shut down a controversial $1.776 billion program known as the “Anti-Weaponization Fund.” The ...

item.title: Black Crowes Singer Chris Robinson Responds to 'U-S-A' Chants During Tampa Concert
item.description: Chris Robinson, the lead singer of The Black Crowes, became involved in a tense exchange with concertgoers during a performance in Tampa, Florida. According to reports, the incident occurred as the ba...

item.title: Nawaz Sharif to meet PML-N leaders in Gilgit-Baltistan ahead of elections
item.description: Former Prime Minister and PML-N leader Nawaz Sharif is scheduled to visit Gilgit-Baltistan on Tuesday for a one-day trip to meet with party leaders and ticket-holders ahead of the June 7 elections, wh...

item.title: Trump Administration Halts $1.8 Billion Fund Amid Republican Oversight Concerns
item.description: The Trump administration has halted its $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund' following a temporary court order blocking it, but the controversy is far from over. Sources indicate that while the De...

item.title: Liam Gallagher appointed ambassador for Brighter Sound and launches charity trainer prize draw
item.description: Liam Gallagher has been announced as the new ambassador for Manchester-based music charity Brighter Sound, a non-profit organisation dedicated to supporting emerging musicians and creative talent. Fou...

item.title: Study finds kitchen sponges release microplastics during normal use, with water consumption as the primary environmental impact
item.description: A new study led by researchers from the University of Bonn investigated how kitchen sponges contribute to microplastic pollution during everyday dishwashing. The research combined laboratory experimen...

item.title: Fire destroys Barnaba Classic's HighTable Sound Entertainment Studios in Dar es Salaam
item.description: The Tanzanian music industry is in mourning after a fire destroyed HighTable Sound Entertainment Studios, owned by Bongo Fleva artist Barnaba Classic. The blaze broke out around 8:30 pm yesterday in K...

item.title: EFCC Calls Fifth Witness in Olu Agunloye’s Mambilla Power Project Case
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) don call their fifth prosecution witness for the trial of former Minister of Power, Olu Agunloye, over the $6 billion Mambilla Power Project contrac...

item.title: Sen. McCormick accidentally compares Trump administration to an authoritarian regime
item.description: During a recent appearance on Fox News' 'America's Newsroom,' Senator David McCormick (R-PA) almost referred to the Trump administration as a 'regime' while defending President Donald Trump's decision...

item.title: Jigawa Governor suspends four aides over alleged party-related disciplinary issues
item.description: The Governor of Jigawa State, Umar Namadi, has approved the indefinite suspension of four of his aides, with the decision taking immediate effect. According to a statement issued by the Secretary to t...

item.title: Court ruling on protest speech raises questions for DOJ case against James Comey
item.description: A recent federal court ruling in an unrelated case has drawn attention to potential implications for the Trump Justice Department’s criminal prosecution of former FBI Director James Comey. The case it...

item.title: A Nigerian Scam Artist’s Rise Through Online Romance Fraud
item.description: Carlos Barragán’s excerpt from 'The Yahoo Boys' tells the story of Chibuike, a young Nigerian man who turned to online romance scams after struggling with low-paying work and minor hustles. Initially ...

item.title: Alexis Wilkins Files Defamation Lawsuit Against MS NOW Over Misleading FBI Story
item.description: Alexis Wilkins, the girlfriend of FBI Director Kash Patel, has filed a defamation lawsuit against the media outlet MS NOW. The lawsuit claims that MS NOW published false and reckless reports about Wil...

item.title: Federal Appeals Court Blocks Pentagon Ban on Transgender Servicemembers
item.description: A federal appeals court has blocked Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's ban on transgender individuals serving in the U.S. military, ruling that the policy is rooted in unconstitutional animus rather tha...

item.title: Government warns on rising smartphone use among children and expands digital safety education
item.description: The Tanzanian government has raised strong concern about the increasing use of smartphones among children, highlighting the risks linked to online abuse and exposure to harmful content. Speaking in Pa...
